title: Hives
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Hives are itchy raised skin bumps, also called urticaria.
Hives can be caused by allergic reactions, but can also occur with infections, stress, or other triggers.
Hives usually go away on their own, but more serious cases may require medicine or a shot.
Airway swelling or trouble breathing with hives is a medical emergency.
